---
id: spa
name: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
length_km: 7.004
country: Belgium
---

Spa is a high-speed circuit with significant elevation change. The
surface holds water in the Ardennes microclimate — weather can shift
mid-lap. Long full-throttle stretches stress engines and rear tyres;
the climb out of Eau Rouge to Les Combes runs nearly 30 seconds at
full load. Default downforce trim is medium-low to keep speed up the
Kemmel straight.

## La Source

Tight right hairpin at T1. Brake from 6th to 2nd around the 100m
board. Late apex — release the brake mid-rotation to keep the front
loaded. Bias slightly forward; the rears unload heavily on entry.

## Eau Rouge / Raidillon

Compression at the bottom, then climbing left-right-left. Flat in
modern dry pace. Do not lift mid-arc — the rear unloads at the crest
and any sudden release of throttle steps it out. Stay left at the exit
to set up Kemmel.

## Les Combes

Heavy braking from top speed into a tight right. Single late apex,
then short straight into a left-right chicane. Tyres are typically at
peak temp here — a useful place to attempt an overtake under braking.

## Pouhon

Long double-left, downhill into the second apex. Single brake input
on entry, no mid-corner lift. Late apex on the second part loads the
fronts and unloads the rears for the kink onto Fagnes. Track-out can
run wide if the front pushes.
